rule.desc: Prefer public attributes for simple stored values over private attributes with `@property` decorators.
rule.rationale: - Type hints provide compile-time encapsulation without runtime overhead (PY125-ENFORCE-IMMUTABILITY-VIA-TYPES) - Properties should be reserved for computed values, validation, or lazy initialization (PZ205-PROPERTY-WITH-SIDE-EFFECTS) - Python's Uniform Access Principle allows refactoring public attributes to properties later without breaking callers - Avoid premature abstraction (YAGNI) - don't add indirection until needed
rule.tool_support: - mypy enforces type hints regardless of public/private - ruff can detect unnecessary properties (consider adding custom rule)

notes.note: - Computed values calculated on access - Validation required on setting (with `@property.setter`) - Lazy initialization (use `@cached_property` from functools) - Mutable state requiring controlled access - Complex logic or side effects on access
- Simple stored values (not computed on access) - Immutable after initialization - No validation or transformation needed - Frequently accessed (zero overhead matters)
